title: Imagined speech EEG dataset — vowels condition (Nguyen et al. 2017)
description: This dataset comprises preprocessed EEG recordings from 8 healthy subjects performing imagined speech tasks involving three vowel phonemes (a, i, u). Subjects received auditory and visual cues to imagine speaking each vowel, with 64-channel EEG data sampled at 256 Hz and analyzed using Riemannian manifold-based feature extraction and relevance vector machine classification. The dataset includes 7,200 trials (2,400 per vowel class) and has been preprocessed with bandpass filtering, notch filtering, and artifact removal.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired using a 64-channel BrainProducts ActiCHamp system (60 EEG channels, 4 EOG channels) at 256 Hz sampling rate with standard 10-20 montage. Preprocessing included 5th-order Butterworth bandpass filtering (8-70 Hz), 60 Hz notch filtering, adaptive EOG artifact removal, and downsampling from 1000 Hz to 256 Hz. Feature extraction employed Riemannian tangent space mapping with common spatial pattern spatial filtering across mu (8-13 Hz), beta (13-30 Hz), and gamma (30-70 Hz) frequency bands.
